response_text:
# Git Branching Strategies: GitFlow vs Trunk-Based Development

## The Core Philosophy Difference

```
GitFlow: Multiple long-lived branches, structured release process
Trunk-Based: Single main branch, continuous integration, short-lived branches
```

---

## GitFlow

### Branch Structure
```
main (production)
│
├── develop (integration branch)
│   │
│   ├── feature/user-auth
│   ├── feature/payment-system
│   └── feature/dashboard
│
├── release/1.2.0
│
└── hotfix/critical-bug
```

### How It Works

```bash
# 1. Start a new feature
git checkout develop
git checkout -b feature/user-auth

# Work on feature...
git add .
git commit -m "Add JWT authentication"

# 2. Finish feature - merge back to develop
git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff feature/user-auth
git branch -d feature/user-auth

# 3. Create release branch when ready
git checkout develop
git checkout -b release/1.2.0

# Fix release bugs, bump version...
git commit -m "Bump version to 1.2.0"

# 4. Finish release - merge to BOTH main and develop
git checkout main
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git tag -a v1.2.0

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git branch -d release/1.2.0

# 5. Hotfix on production
git checkout main
git checkout -b hotfix/payment-crash

git commit -m "Fix null pointer in payment"

git checkout main
git merge --no-ff hotfix/payment-crash
git tag -a v1.2.1

git checkout develop          # Don't forget develop!
git merge --no-ff hotfix/payment-crash
```

### GitFlow Branch Rules
```
Branch          | Source    | Merges Into      | Purpose
----------------|-----------|------------------|------------------
main            | -         | -                | Production code
develop         | main      | -                | Integration
feature/*       | develop
